Math4121Introduction to Lebesgue Integration (Lecture 9)

Math4121 Lecture 9

Exam next week.

Transition to new book.

Continue on Chapter 6

Integrable Functions

Theorem 6.11

Suppose f∈R(α)f\in \mathscr{R}(\alpha)f∈R(α) on [a,b][a, b][a,b], m≤f(x)≤Mm\leq f(x)\leq Mm≤f(x)≤M for all x∈[a,b]x\in [a, b]x∈[a,b], and ϕ\phiϕ is continuous on [m,M][m, M][m,M], and let h(x)=ϕ(f(x))h(x)=\phi(f(x))h(x)=ϕ(f(x)) on [a,b][a, b][a,b]. Then h∈R(α)h\in \mathscr{R}(\alpha)h∈R(α) on [a,b][a, b][a,b].

Proof:

Since ϕ\phiϕ is uniformly continuous on [m,M][m, M][m,M], for any ϵ>0\epsilon > 0ϵ>0, there exists a δ>0\delta > 0δ>0 such that if s,t∈[m,M]s, t\in [m, M]s,t∈[m,M] and ∣s−t∣<δ|s-t| < \delta∣s−t∣<δ, then ∣ϕ(s)−ϕ(t)∣<ϵ|\phi(s)-\phi(t)| < \epsilon∣ϕ(s)−ϕ(t)∣<ϵ.

Since f∈R(α)f\in \mathscr{R}(\alpha)f∈R(α) on [a,b][a, b][a,b], we can find a partition P={x0,x1,⋯ ,xn}P=\{x_0, x_1, \cdots, x_n\}P={x0​,x1​,⋯,xn​} of [a,b][a, b][a,b] such that U(f,P,α)−L(f,P,α)<ϵδU(f, P, \alpha)-L(f, P, \alpha) < \epsilon \deltaU(f,P,α)−L(f,P,α)<ϵδ.

Set Mi=sup⁡{f(x):x∈[xi−1,xi]}M_i=\sup \{f(x): x\in [x_{i-1}, x_i]\}Mi​=sup{f(x):x∈[xi−1​,xi​]} and mi=inf⁡{f(x):x∈[xi−1,xi]}m_i=\inf \{f(x): x\in [x_{i-1}, x_i]\}mi​=inf{f(x):x∈[xi−1​,xi​]}. Mi∗=sup⁡{h(x):x∈[xi−1,xi]}M_i^*=\sup \{h(x): x\in [x_{i-1}, x_i]\}Mi∗​=sup{h(x):x∈[xi−1​,xi​]} and mi∗=inf⁡{h(x):x∈[xi−1,xi]}m_i^*=\inf \{h(x): x\in [x_{i-1}, x_i]\}mi∗​=inf{h(x):x∈[xi−1​,xi​]}.

We call a index iii good if Mi−mi<δM_i-m_i < \deltaMi​−mi​<δ.

If iii is good, then ∀x,y∈[xi−1,xi]\forall x, y\in [x_{i-1}, x_i]∀x,y∈[xi−1​,xi​], ∣f(x)−f(y)∣<δ|f(x)-f(y)| < \delta∣f(x)−f(y)∣<δ and by uniform continuity of ϕ\phiϕ, ∣ϕ(f(x))−ϕ(f(y))∣<ϵ|\phi(f(x))-\phi(f(y))| < \epsilon∣ϕ(f(x))−ϕ(f(y))∣<ϵ.

Therefore, ∣Mi∗−mi∗∣<ϵ|M_i^*-m_i^*| < \epsilon∣Mi∗​−mi∗​∣<ϵ.

If iii is bad, then Mi−mi≥δM_i-m_i\geq \deltaMi​−mi​≥δ.

Notice that

δ∑i∈badΔαi≤∑i∈bad(Mi−mi)Δαi≤∑i=1n(Mi−mi)Δαi≤U(f,P,α)−L(f,P,α)<ϵδ\begin{aligned} \delta\sum_{i\in\text{bad}} \Delta \alpha_i &\leq \sum_{i\in\text{bad}} (M_i-m_i) \Delta \alpha_i \\ &\leq \sum_{i=1}^n (M_i-m_i) \Delta \alpha_i \\ &\leq U(f, P, \alpha)-L(f, P, \alpha) \\ &< \epsilon\delta \end{aligned}δi∈bad∑​Δαi​​≤i∈bad∑​(Mi​−mi​)Δαi​≤i=1∑n​(Mi​−mi​)Δαi​≤U(f,P,α)−L(f,P,α)<ϵδ​

Therefore, ∑i=1n(Mi∗−mi∗)Δαi<ϵ\sum_{i=1}^n (M_i^*-m_i^*) \Delta \alpha_i < \epsilon∑i=1n​(Mi∗​−mi∗​)Δαi​<ϵ.

So,

U(P,h,α)−L(P,h,α)=∑i=1n(Mi∗−mi∗)Δαi≤∑i∈goodϵΔαi+∑i∈bad2sup⁡{∣h(x)−h(y)∣:x,y∈[xi−1,xi]}Δαi≤ϵ[α(b)−α(a)]+2ϵsup⁡{∣h(x)−h(y)∣:x,y∈[a,b]}\begin{aligned} U(P,h,\alpha)-L(P,h,\alpha) &= \sum_{i=1}^n (M_i^*-m_i^*) \Delta \alpha_i \\ &\leq \sum_{i\in\text{good}} \epsilon \Delta \alpha_i + \sum_{i\in\text{bad}}2 \sup \{|h(x)-h(y)|: x, y\in [x_{i-1}, x_i]\} \Delta \alpha_i \\ &\leq \epsilon [\alpha(b)-\alpha(a)] + 2\epsilon \sup \{|h(x)-h(y)|: x, y\in [a, b]\}\\ \end{aligned}U(P,h,α)−L(P,h,α)​=i=1∑n​(Mi∗​−mi∗​)Δαi​≤i∈good∑​ϵΔαi​+i∈bad∑​2sup{∣h(x)−h(y)∣:x,y∈[xi−1​,xi​]}Δαi​≤ϵ[α(b)−α(a)]+2ϵsup{∣h(x)−h(y)∣:x,y∈[a,b]}​

Since ϵ\epsilonϵ is arbitrary, h∈R(α)h\in \mathscr{R}(\alpha)h∈R(α) on [a,b][a, b][a,b].

QED

Properties of Integrable Functions

Theorem 6.12

Let f,g∈R(α)f,g\in \mathscr{R}(\alpha)f,g∈R(α) on [a,b][a, b][a,b].

(a) f+g∈R(α)f+g\in \mathscr{R}(\alpha)f+g∈R(α) on [a,b][a, b][a,b], ∫ab(f+g)dα=∫abfdα+∫abgdα\int_a^b (f+g)d\alpha = \int_a^b f d\alpha + \int_a^b g d\alpha∫ab​(f+g)dα=∫ab​fdα+∫ab​gdα. (Linearity of the integral)

(aa) If c∈Rc\in \mathbb{R}c∈R, then cf∈R(α)cf\in \mathscr{R}(\alpha)cf∈R(α) on [a,b][a, b][a,b], and ∫abcfdα=c∫abfdα\int_a^b cf d\alpha = c\int_a^b f d\alpha∫ab​cfdα=c∫ab​fdα.

(b) If f(x)≤g(x),∀x∈[a,b]f(x)\leq g(x),\forall x\in [a, b]f(x)≤g(x),∀x∈[a,b], then ∫abfdα≤∫abgdα\int_a^b f d\alpha \leq \int_a^b g d\alpha∫ab​fdα≤∫ab​gdα.

(c) c∈[a,b]c\in [a, b]c∈[a,b], then ∫acfdα+∫cbfdα=∫abfdα\int_a^c f d\alpha + \int_c^b f d\alpha = \int_a^b f d\alpha∫ac​fdα+∫cb​fdα=∫ab​fdα.

(d) If ∣f(x)∣≤M|f(x)|\leq M∣f(x)∣≤M, then ∣∫abfdα∣≤M(α(b)−α(a))|\int_a^b f d\alpha| \leq M(\alpha(b)-\alpha(a))∣∫ab​fdα∣≤M(α(b)−α(a)).

(e) If f∈R(β)f\in \mathscr{R}(\beta)f∈R(β) then f∈R(α+β)f\in \mathscr{R}(\alpha+\beta)f∈R(α+β) and ∫abfd(α+β)=∫abfdα+∫abfdβ\int_a^b f d(\alpha+\beta) = \int_a^b f d\alpha + \int_a^b f d\beta∫ab​fd(α+β)=∫ab​fdα+∫ab​fdβ.

Proof:

Property (aa), (b), (e) holds for Riemann Sums themselves.

For (a), Set h(x)=f(x)+g(x)h(x)=f(x)+g(x)h(x)=f(x)+g(x). Then h∈R(α)h\in \mathscr{R}(\alpha)h∈R(α) on [a,b][a, b][a,b] and we will show ∫abhdα≤∫abfdα+∫abgdα\int_a^b h d\alpha \leq \int_a^b f d\alpha + \int_a^b g d\alpha∫ab​hdα≤∫ab​fdα+∫ab​gdα.

Since f,g∈R(α)f,g\in \mathscr{R}(\alpha)f,g∈R(α) on [a,b][a, b][a,b], for any ϵ>0\epsilon > 0ϵ>0, there exists a partition P1,P2P_1,P_2P1​,P2​ of [a,b][a, b][a,b] such that U(f,P1,α)−L(f,P1,α)<ϵU(f,P_1,\alpha)-L(f,P_1,\alpha) < \epsilonU(f,P1​,α)−L(f,P1​,α)<ϵ and U(g,P2,α)−L(g,P2,α)<ϵU(g,P_2,\alpha)-L(g,P_2,\alpha) < \epsilonU(g,P2​,α)−L(g,P2​,α)<ϵ.

Let P=P1∪P2P=P_1\cup P_2P=P1​∪P2​. Then U(P,f,α)≤U(P1,f,α)<∫abfdα+ϵU(P,f,\alpha)\leq U(P_1,f,\alpha)< \int_a^b f d\alpha + \epsilonU(P,f,α)≤U(P1​,f,α)<∫ab​fdα+ϵ and U(P,g,α)≤U(P2,g,α)<∫abgdα+ϵU(P,g,\alpha)\leq U(P_2,g,\alpha)< \int_a^b g d\alpha + \epsilonU(P,g,α)≤U(P2​,g,α)<∫ab​gdα+ϵ.

So U(P,h,α)≤U(P,f,α)+U(P,g,α)≤∫abfdα+∫abgdα+2ϵU(P,h,\alpha)\leq U(P,f,\alpha)+U(P,g,\alpha)\leq \int_a^b f d\alpha + \int_a^b g d\alpha + 2\epsilonU(P,h,α)≤U(P,f,α)+U(P,g,α)≤∫ab​fdα+∫ab​gdα+2ϵ.

Since ϵ\epsilonϵ is arbitrary, ∫abhdα≤∫abfdα+∫abgdα\int_a^b h d\alpha \leq \int_a^b f d\alpha + \int_a^b g d\alpha∫ab​hdα≤∫ab​fdα+∫ab​gdα.

sup⁡cf(x)=csup⁡f(x)∀c∈R\sup cf(x) = c\sup f(x)\quad \forall c\in \mathbb{R}supcf(x)=csupf(x)∀c∈R U(P,cf,α)=cU(P,f,α)U(P,cf, \alpha) = cU(P,f,\alpha)U(P,cf,α)=cU(P,f,α)

For (b), notice that if f(x)≤g(x)f(x)\leq g(x)f(x)≤g(x), then sup⁡f(x)≤sup⁡g(x)\sup f(x)\leq \sup g(x)supf(x)≤supg(x), U(P,f,α)≤U(P,g,α)U(P,f,\alpha)\leq U(P,g,\alpha)U(P,f,α)≤U(P,g,α). and L(P,f,α)≤L(P,g,α)L(P,f,\alpha)\leq L(P,g,\alpha)L(P,f,α)≤L(P,g,α).

For (c), if f∈R(α)f\in \mathscr{R}(\alpha)f∈R(α) on [a,b][a,b][a,b], and if a<c<ba<c<ba<c<b, then f∈Rf\in \mathscr{R}f∈R on [a,c][a,c][a,c] and [c,b][c,b][c,b], and

∫acfdα+∫cbfdα=∫abfdα\int_a^c f d\alpha + \int_c^b f d\alpha=\int_a^b f d\alpha∫ac​fdα+∫cb​fdα=∫ab​fdα

For every partition P={x0,x1,⋯ ,xn}P=\{x_0,x_1,\cdots,x_n\}P={x0​,x1​,⋯,xn​} of [a,b][a,b][a,b], we have a refinement P∗=P∪{c}P^*=P\cup\{c\}P∗=P∪{c} of [a,b][a,b][a,b]. Let P1={x0,x1,⋯ ,xj,c}P_1=\{x_0,x_1,\cdots,x_j,c\}P1​={x0​,x1​,⋯,xj​,c} and P2={c,xj+1,⋯ ,xn}P_2=\{c,x_{j+1},\cdots,x_n\}P2​={c,xj+1​,⋯,xn​} be the partitions of [a,c][a,c][a,c] and [c,b][c,b][c,b] respectively. So

U(P∗,f,α)=∑i=0nMi(xi−xi+1)=Mc(c−xj)+∑i=0j−1Mi(xi−xi+1)+∑i=j+1nMi(xi−xi+1)=U(P1,f,α)+U(P2,f,α)\begin{aligned} U(P^*,f,\alpha)&=\sum_{i=0}^{n}M_i(x_i-x_{i+1})\\ &=M_c(c-x_j)+\sum_{i=0}^{j-1}M_i(x_i-x_{i+1})+\sum_{i=j+1}^{n}M_i(x_i-x_{i+1})\\ &=U(P_1,f,\alpha)+U(P_2,f,\alpha) \end{aligned}U(P∗,f,α)​=i=0∑n​Mi​(xi​−xi+1​)=Mc​(c−xj​)+i=0∑j−1​Mi​(xi​−xi+1​)+i=j+1∑n​Mi​(xi​−xi+1​)=U(P1​,f,α)+U(P2​,f,α)​

and

L(P∗,f,α)=∑i=0nmi(xi−xi+1)=mc(xj−c)+∑i=0j−1mi(xi−xi+1)+∑i=j+1n−1mi(xi−xi+1)=L(P1,f,α)+L(P2,f,α)\begin{aligned} L(P^*,f,\alpha)&=\sum_{i=0}^{n}m_i(x_i-x_{i+1})\\ &=m_c(x_j-c)+\sum_{i=0}^{j-1}m_i(x_i-x_{i+1})+\sum_{i=j+1}^{n-1}m_i(x_i-x_{i+1})\\ &=L(P_1,f,\alpha)+L(P_2,f,\alpha) \end{aligned}L(P∗,f,α)​=i=0∑n​mi​(xi​−xi+1​)=mc​(xj​−c)+i=0∑j−1​mi​(xi​−xi+1​)+i=j+1∑n−1​mi​(xi​−xi+1​)=L(P1​,f,α)+L(P2​,f,α)​

Since P∗P^*P∗ is a refinement of PPP, by \textbf{Theorem 6.4}, we have U(P∗,f,α)≤U(P,f,α)U(P^*,f,\alpha)\leq U(P,f,\alpha)U(P∗,f,α)≤U(P,f,α) and L(P∗,f,α)≥L(P,f,α)L(P^*,f,\alpha)\geq L(P,f,\alpha)L(P∗,f,α)≥L(P,f,α).

So ∫acfdα+∫cbfdα≤U(P∗,f,α)≤U(P,f,α)=∫abfdα\int_a^c f d\alpha+\int_c^b f d\alpha\leq U(P^*,f,\alpha)\leq U(P,f,\alpha)=\int_a^b f d\alpha∫ac​fdα+∫cb​fdα≤U(P∗,f,α)≤U(P,f,α)=∫ab​fdα.

Similarly, we have ∫acfdα+∫cbfdα≥L(P∗,f,α)≥L(P,f,α)=∫abfdα\int_a^c f d\alpha+\int_c^b f d\alpha\geq L(P^*,f,\alpha)\geq L(P,f,\alpha)=\int_a^b f d\alpha∫ac​fdα+∫cb​fdα≥L(P∗,f,α)≥L(P,f,α)=∫ab​fdα.

Therefore, ∫acfdα+∫cbfdα=∫abfdα\int_a^c f d\alpha+\int_c^b f d\alpha=\int_a^b f d\alpha∫ac​fdα+∫cb​fdα=∫ab​fdα.

For (d), if f∈R(α)f\in \mathscr{R}(\alpha)f∈R(α) on [a,b][a,b][a,b], and if ∣f(x)∣≤M|f(x)| \leq M∣f(x)∣≤M on [a,b][a,b][a,b], then

∣∫abfdα∣≤M(α(b)−α(a))\left|\int_a^b f d\alpha\right| \leq M(\alpha(b)-\alpha(a))​∫ab​fdα​≤M(α(b)−α(a))

Since ∣f(x)∣≤M|f(x)|\leq M∣f(x)∣≤M on [a,b][a,b][a,b], ∀x∈[a,b]\forall x\in [a,b]∀x∈[a,b], we have f(x)∈[−M,M]f(x)\in [-M,M]f(x)∈[−M,M] on [a,b][a,b][a,b]. So sup⁡∣f(x)∣≤M\sup|f(x)|\leq Msup∣f(x)∣≤M and inf⁡∣f(x)∣≤M\inf|f(x)|\leq Minf∣f(x)∣≤M. Since L(P,f,α)≤∫abfdα≤U(P,f,α)L(P,f,\alpha)\leq \int_a^b f d\alpha\leq U(P,f,\alpha)L(P,f,α)≤∫ab​fdα≤U(P,f,α), we have

So

∣∫abfdα∣≤max⁡{∣L(P,f,α)∣,∣U(P,f,α)∣}=max⁡{∑i=0n−1∣Mi∣Δxi,∑i=0n−1∣mi∣Δxi}≤∑i=0n−1max⁡{∣Mi∣,∣mi∣}Δxi≤∑i=0n−1MΔxi=M(α(b)−α(a))\begin{aligned} \left|\int_a^b f d\alpha\right|&\leq \max\left\{|L(P,f,\alpha)|,|U(P,f,\alpha)|\right\}\\ &=\max\left\{\sum_{i=0}^{n-1}|M_i|\Delta x_i,\sum_{i=0}^{n-1}|m_i|\Delta x_i\right\}\\ &\leq \sum_{i=0}^{n-1}\max\{|M_i|,|m_i|\}\Delta x_i\\ &\leq \sum_{i=0}^{n-1}M\Delta x_i\\ &=M(\alpha(b)-\alpha(a)) \end{aligned}​∫ab​fdα​​≤max{∣L(P,f,α)∣,∣U(P,f,α)∣}=max{i=0∑n−1​∣Mi​∣Δxi​,i=0∑n−1​∣mi​∣Δxi​}≤i=0∑n−1​max{∣Mi​∣,∣mi​∣}Δxi​≤i=0∑n−1​MΔxi​=M(α(b)−α(a))​

Therefore, ∣∫abfdα∣≤M(α(b)−α(a))\left|\int_a^b f d\alpha\right| \leq M(\alpha(b)-\alpha(a))​∫ab​fdα​≤M(α(b)−α(a)).

For (e), notice that

Δ(α+β)i=α(xi)−α(xi−1)+β(xi)−β(xi−1)=Δαi+Δβi\begin{aligned} \Delta (\alpha+\beta)_i &= \alpha(x_i)-\alpha(x_{i-1})+\beta(x_i)-\beta(x_{i-1}) \\ &= \Delta \alpha_i + \Delta \beta_i \end{aligned}Δ(α+β)i​​=α(xi​)−α(xi−1​)+β(xi​)−β(xi−1​)=Δαi​+Δβi​​

QED
